name: Tennessee
address: 312 Rosa L. Parks Avenue, 6th Floor, Snodgrass Tower, Nashville, TN 37243-1102

llc.formName: Articles of Termination of Limited Liability Company
steps: Vote to dissolve the LLC according to the operating agreement
File Notice of Dissolution (Form SS-4246) with the Secretary of State — $20 fee
Wind up business affairs and settle all debts and obligations
Obtain tax clearance from the Tennessee Department of Revenue
File Articles of Termination (Form SS-4245) with the Secretary of State — $20 fee
Cancel any licenses, permits, and registrations
File final state and federal tax returns
Close business bank accounts and cancel your EIN with the IRS
llc.notes: Tennessee requires two separate filings: a Notice of Dissolution ($20) and Articles of Termination ($20), totaling $40. You must obtain tax clearance from the Department of Revenue before filing Articles of Termination. Missing the termination filing leaves the LLC on the books and subject to the $300 annual report fee.

steps: Hold a board of directors meeting to recommend dissolution
Obtain shareholder approval for the dissolution
Wind up business affairs and settle all debts and obligations
Obtain tax clearance from the Tennessee Department of Revenue
File Articles of Dissolution (Form SS-4410) with the Secretary of State — $20 fee
Cancel any licenses, permits, and registrations
File final state and federal tax returns
Close business bank accounts and cancel your EIN with the IRS
corporation.notes: The Division of Business Services will request tax clearance from the Department of Revenue when you file. If outstanding tax debt exists, the articles will be rejected.

tax.notes: Tax clearance is required before filing Articles of Termination. Request tax clearance through TNTAP (Tennessee Taxpayer Access Point). If all taxes are paid, the Department of Revenue will mail a tax clearance letter within 3-5 days and send an electronic copy to the Secretary of State. Tennessee has franchise and excise taxes that must be settled.

timeline.expeditedProcessingDays: Same day or next business day

requirements.employeeNotification: Must provide WARN Act notice (60 days) if 100+ employees
requirements.creditorNotification: Must notify known creditors of the dissolution and provide a reasonable deadline for claims (minimum 120 days)
requirements.registeredAgentNotes: Registered agent designation terminates upon final dissolution filing
requirements.boardOrMemberApproval: LLC: Member vote as specified in operating agreement. Corporation: Board resolution followed by shareholder approval (majority of shares entitled to vote).

includes: Every required form with direct download links
